metadata.subject: Arts Education
metadata.description: Dance Technique and Performance 12 is the capstone senior dance course, developing students' highest level of technical proficiency, performance artistry, choreographic sophistication, and critical understanding. Students engage in the full arc of professional dance practice — from rigorous training and rehearsal to polished performance and critical reflection. First Peoples dance knowledge, diverse global traditions, and contemporary dance landscapes are studied in depth. This course prepares students for post-secondary dance, performance, and movement-based programs.
metadata.general_description: The capstone senior dance course developing the highest level of technique, expressive artistry, choreographic skill, and cultural understanding — preparing students for post-secondary dance and performance pathways.

big_ideas.statement: Artistic mastery integrates technical skill, expressive depth, and cultural understanding.
big_ideas.elaboration: At the highest level, dance artistry is not only about physical technique — it requires emotional intelligence, cultural awareness, and the ability to communicate complex meaning through movement.

big_ideas.statement: Dancers are cultural agents whose work carries meaning in and for the world.
big_ideas.elaboration: Professional dance practice carries responsibilities — to traditions, to communities, and to the audiences whose understanding is shaped by what they witness. Dancers make choices that matter beyond the studio.

big_ideas.statement: Sustained artistic development requires commitment, vulnerability, and a growth mindset.
big_ideas.elaboration: Reaching the highest levels of dance artistry requires years of consistent, reflective practice — combined with the courage to keep taking risks, seeking feedback, and growing beyond current limits.

competencies.raw: Demonstrate the highest level of technical skill and expressive range; create sophisticated choreographic works; take purposeful, well-developed artistic risks.
i_can_statements: I can demonstrate the highest level of technical skill and full expressive range in my performance.
I can create sophisticated original choreographic works with clear artistic vision and cultural awareness.
I can take well-developed, purposeful creative risks that reflect a mature artistic voice.

competencies.raw: Critically analyze dance works and own performance using sophisticated vocabulary; lead artistic reflection and feedback processes; examine dance's role in culture, society, and history.
i_can_statements: I can critically analyze dance works — including my own — using sophisticated analytical vocabulary and frameworks.
I can lead artistic reflection and give constructive, specific feedback to peers.
I can articulate dance's role in cultural transmission, social change, and community identity.

competencies.raw: Perform at a high artistic level in varied and formal contexts; create a professional-quality artistic portfolio; communicate artistic vision to audiences.
i_can_statements: I can perform at a high artistic level in formal concert, studio, and community settings.
I can develop a professional-quality artistic portfolio documenting technical and artistic development.
I can clearly communicate my artistic vision, intent, and process to audiences and peers.

competencies.raw: Lead by example in professionalism and ensemble culture; engage with First Peoples dance as part of living cultural practice; prepare for post-secondary dance pathways.
i_can_statements: I can lead by example in rehearsal and performance culture — demonstrating professionalism, care, and commitment.
I can engage with First Peoples dance as a living, evolving cultural practice with deep respect and understanding.
I can prepare thoughtfully for post-secondary dance auditions, training programs, and career pathways.

areas.name: Capstone Technical Training
areas.raw: Highest-level technique in one or more dance disciplines.
topics: Style-specific training at the highest level: technique, nuance, and stylistic authenticity
Advanced injury prevention: anatomy, kinesiology, and sustainable practice
Cross-training: Pilates, yoga, Gyrokinesis, or other movement modalities
Video analysis: reviewing own performance and applying specific improvements
Audition preparation: technique, repertoire, and professional presentation

areas.name: Capstone Performance and Production
areas.raw: Concert production, full-length performance work, and professional practice.
topics: Full-length concert production: contributing to all aspects of production
Repertoire performance: performing complex, multi-section choreographic works
Technical production: lights, sound, costumes, and stage management contributions
Professional rehearsal culture: call sheets, performance schedules, and company etiquette
Post-performance critical reflection and documentation

areas.name: Choreographic Capstone
areas.raw: Creating a substantial original choreographic work.
topics: Developing a choreographic concept: research, artistic statement, and vision
Creating a full-length work: structure, movement development, and narrative arc
Casting and working with performers
Technical elements: designing with lighting, sound, and visual elements
Public presentation and artist talk

areas.name: Dance Studies and Post-Secondary Preparation
areas.raw: Dance history, research, and post-secondary pathways.
topics: In-depth dance history and theory: feminist, postcolonial, and decolonial approaches to dance studies
First Peoples dance scholarship: learning from Indigenous dance scholars and artists
Dance research: writing a substantial analytical paper or artist's statement
Post-secondary pathways: dance programs, audition preparation, and career options
Dance and community: ongoing engagement as a dancer-citizen
